item.name: Blooded Wrasse
item.description:
Fishers should not feel guilty for the bloodstains on the fish's head, as these are largely self-inflicted. They can instead feel guilty about the hook through the mouth, as well as the gutting and cleaning that comes shortly after.

[Suitable for printing on large canvases.]

fish.guide: This species of wrasse does, in fact, owe its name to the Bloodbrine Sea in which it dwells. It was merely a happy coincidence that the habit these wavekin have of smashing their lumpy heads together often gives them a scarlet-stained appearance.
